title: Mitigate inference latency after cache regression
summary: Use cache hit rate, worker saturation, and deploy diff analysis to isolate post-release latency spikes.
steps: Compare p50 / p95 / p99 metrics before and after the release.
Inspect cache hit rate and request queue depth for the affected service.
Throttle traffic or rollback if worker saturation continues to rise.
Validate autoscaling, concurrency limits, and dependency latencies.

title: Recover from secret rotation or auth token failure
summary: Validate issued tokens, secret distribution, and downstream permissions after auth-related failures.
steps: Check recent secret rotations and confirm the active version in the failing service.
Inspect IAM or RBAC changes for the principal used by the service.
Rotate or refresh tokens and retry a single canary request.
Audit downstream 401 / 403 responses to isolate the failing dependency.

title: Contain memory pressure in model serving workloads
summary: Diagnose memory regressions caused by larger model footprints, batch size changes, or cache retention.
steps: Inspect container RSS and GPU memory usage over the last deployment window.
Reduce batch size, prefetch depth, or cache retention until the service stabilizes.
Look for recent model or tokenizer changes that increase resident footprint.
Rollback the deployment if eviction or OOM kill events continue.

title: Investigate network connectivity and DNS failures
summary: Use dependency error rates, name resolution metrics, and connection traces to isolate connectivity issues.
steps: Check DNS resolution timing and service mesh or load balancer health.
Validate egress rules, certificates, and endpoint reachability.
Inspect timeout distribution and dependency-specific failure concentration.
Fail over or route around unhealthy endpoints if available.
